type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The Mining Associate of Canada conducted a comparative review of reported NPRI emissions from various mining facilities across Canada.  This review indicated that Teck Coal’s reported particulate emissions were high relative to other operations.  Teck preformed a detailed review of their calculations to identify areas of conservatism within their emission calculations, with the intention of improving the calculated values to more accurately reflect the releases.  As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  For the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock. 
 
Additionally, previous estimates for fugitive dust from stockpiles, dumps, and other exposed areas conservatively assumed that the entire area was active, with activity from bulldozers and other equipment as well as wind erosion.  In order to reduce conservatisms for the 2023 reporting year, it was assumed that 10% of waste rock and CCR were active and 0% of all other area types were active.  The inactive areas were assumed to only be impacted by wind erosion.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment:
The Mining Associate of Canada conducted a comparative review of reported NPRI emissions from various mining facilities across Canada.  This review indicated that Teck Coal’s reported particulate emissions were high relative to other operations.  Teck preformed a detailed review of their calculations to identify areas of conservatism within their emission calculations, with the intention of improving the calculated values to more accurately reflect the releases.  As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  For the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock. 
 
Additionally, previous estimates for fugitive dust from stockpiles, dumps, and other exposed areas conservatively assumed that the entire area was active, with activity from bulldozers and other equipment as well as wind erosion.  In order to reduce conservatisms for the 2023 reporting year, it was assumed that 10% of waste rock and CCR were active and 0% of all other area types were active.  The inactive areas were assumed to only be impacted by wind erosion.

comments.comment:
The Mining Associate of Canada conducted a comparative review of reported NPRI emissions from various mining facilities across Canada.  This review indicated that Teck Coal’s reported particulate emissions were high relative to other operations.  Teck preformed a detailed review of their calculations to identify areas of conservatism within their emission calculations, with the intention of improving the calculated values to more accurately reflect the releases.  As a result of this review, the following changes were made, which resulted in significant decreases in the quantity of fugitive dust reported for the 2023 reporting year.

Previous estimates for fugitive dust were calculated using site specific values for silt and moisture content for coal and waste rock.  For the 2023 reporting year, it was determined that the site-specific silt content does not represent the proper size fraction range for use with U.S. EPA AP-42 calculations.  Therefore, the U.S. EPA AP-42 typical mean values for silt and moisture content for western surface coal mining were used for coal and waste rock. 
 
Additionally, previous estimates for fugitive dust from stockpiles, dumps, and other exposed areas conservatively assumed that the entire area was active, with activity from bulldozers and other equipment as well as wind erosion.  In order to reduce conservatisms for the 2023 reporting year, it was assumed that 10% of waste rock and CCR were active and 0% of all other area types were active.  The inactive areas were assumed to only be impacted by wind erosion.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site

comments.comment: The main driver in the decrease in particulate emissions from CMM is a change in the stockpile sources.  Since CMM is closed and in maintenance mode, outdoor stockpile areas were considered inactive, and emissions were calculated based on emission factors from AP-42 chapter 11.9, for wind erosion of exposed areas, as opposed to previous years which considered emissions based on active stockpiles. Decreased dozer hours , distance travelled by vehicles on unpaved road, and annual average wind speed, as well as an increased number of rainy days and snow cover in 2022 also helped reduce particulate releases.

type.fr: Commentaire sur les rejets sur le site
